name: Murdock
notes:
- Wears a dog tag, which he keeps it hidden on his person at all costs. Claims it contains his name, Murdock. - A slow grower at creation, currently physically stunted from years of scarcity. Young adult, but their voice would make you think otherwise. - Primarily carnivorous, but capable of digesting other types of food. Will eat wings WITH the bone (her stomach is extra fucking acidic). - Likewise, capable of holding breath up to 15min. - Height:

  1. [Solstice Rain]: 4'3" // 1.30m
  2. [Winter Scar]: 4'4" // 1.33m


- [union pilots, currently working for them]


1x Extra Rations gear
history:
Life was sheltered, sure. They were never allowed to leave the labs and meet people they spot through cameras and tech, yeah. They were never allowed to leave the premises, undeniably. But for what it's worth, Murdock remembers their childhood with fondness and comfort - something which is sorely absent in the present day. All it took was a single day for the operatives to break and enter its home, slaughter its father and either ransack or destroy his work and projects, something the hybrid entity took no mind to when it needed to flee to survive. A single day to cast it into the lowest pits and tightest passages, to escape would-be captors and avoid detection.A single day to turn fascination and curiosity to hurt and bitterness.


Perseverance. Adaptation. Spite. Survival was his main objective and desperation his drive, it did not take long for Murdock to turn to petty thievery, vandalism and break-ins of his own to attain what he needs to live another day. Hiding at day and using nights for cover, never seeking help as none were spared for him.With age, experience and stolen manuals, the hybrid became a tinkerer in his spare time, using cobbled together explosives to further terrorize the local districts above, both for ease of access and swift escape. Any discarded appliances becoming either dismantled materials or given a second life for a little longer.


Of course, this wouldn’t exist if they remained hidden. Unbeknownst to him, the local rumors eventually reached the Union, leading some operatives to crack down on their presence. A particularly bad day, and Murdock finds themselves cornered in one of their many passageways, surrounded on all fronts. And yet.A sharp scrap pressed against the throat and threats to claim a life is all it took to be acknowledged as more than a vermin . Or maybe he was just lucky the person whose life he threatened had a soft heart, as his father’s was.


A leap into the unknown a second time. A new turn, a shiny new collar to haphazardly mask her, and promises of a new life, a.. “better” life. Between the choice of death and becoming a pilot, he probably should’ve contemplated more on it. For as much as the prospect of being assigned a mech, a companion of his own, convinced him to join, being suddenly thrust into a compact naval carrier, packed with people (who probably don’t want to acknowledge him as much as he doesn’t want to acknowledge them), it is not something he was keen on. Far from it, actually.…At the very least, she can call Sarchosuchus her own. Wouldn’t be the first time she fixed someone else’s leftovers, after all.

reserves.name: High-Mobility Pack
reserves.label: Resource
reserves.description: The LSA uses these externally mounted flight units for rapid assault and air-drop operations, and the schematics are easy enough to modify and print to GMS standard. You can spend this reserve at the start of any combat to count any and all of your movement as flying for the rest of that scene. Additionally, you may deploy yourself anywhere on the map after all other characters (PCs and NPCs) have deployed.

actions.name: Activate High-Mobility Pack
actions.detail: Spend this reserve at the start of combat. All your movement counts as flying for the rest of that scene. You may deploy yourself anywhere on the map after all other characters have deployed.

MechSystems.name: Pattern-C Advanced Charges
MechSystems.effect: Expend a charge from this system for one of the following effects:
MechSystems.description: GMS’s latest intelligent munition system is a self-contained, multi-mode weapon designed to be easily deployable from a variety of chassis. Boasting advanced decision-making capabilities, each drone-tier charge is capable of independent target selection and interception, maintaining regular pilot-in-the-loop protocols to ensure optimal detonation parameters.

actions.name: Smart Grenade
actions.detail: All hostile characters within the affected area must pass a Systems save or take 1d6 energy damage and gain Lock On. On a success, they gain Lock On only.

deployables.name: Lurker Mine
deployables.detail: This mine does not detonate as normal. Instead, gain the Selective Detonation reaction, which can be performed once per deployed lurker mine.

actions.name: Selective Detonation
actions.detail: The mine detonates and affected characters must pass an Agility save or take 2d6 explosive damage. On a success, they take half damage.
actions.trigger: A character enters the lurker mine’s space or an adjacent space, or begins their turn there.

MechSystems.name: Miniaturized LinAc Coherent Beam Cannon
MechSystems.effect: Your mech gains an additional Core System that can be used 1/mission.
MechSystems.description: Cressidium’s self-imposed isolation has led to a number of divergent technological advances over the centuries, among them the pursuit of more compact lance weapon development as opposed to further exploration and refinement of naval long-cycle weaponry. Weapons engineers looked to miniaturization, incorporating chemical and exotic-metal batteries and rapid-charge fire-cycling systems suitable for use on smaller and smaller platforms, ultimately including mechanized chassis. This example, reverse-engineered from combat data gathered by Union pilots during an engagement against Vestan Sovereignty special forces, appears to be a descendant of old linear accelerator coherent beam cannon designs – weapons now considered out of date by contemporary naval standards but which are nonetheless exceedingly lethal when employed at this scale.

actions.name: LinAc CBC
actions.detail:
Activate this devastating charged particle cannon, firing it in a Line 30 path. All sources of cover, terrain, objects, and deployables smaller than Size 5 in this area are boiled away instantly, and then all characters within the area must pass an Agility save or take 14 energy damage. On a success, they take half damage. This damage can’t be reduced in any way. Characters with 7 HP or less, only 1 Structure remaining, and no Immunity to damage are instead instantly and automatically destroyed, annihilated utterly and leaving no wreck behind.
You can take no other actions on the same turn you activate this particle cannon except for your standard move or Boost, and the stress of firing it causes you to become Stunned until the start of your next turn.

armor.note: When he REALLY doesn't want to be seen.

weapons.note: A mark of an eye is carved onto its blade.

gear.note: The collar stays *on* when you leave your private quarters, Murdock.
Affixed to his boots at all times. Somehow found a way to attach and hotwire a smaller pair to his gloves for further maneuverability on mechs.

systems.flavorDescription: Autoloader drones are many-limbed machines that assist their team by loading ordnance, maintaining powerline hookups, and cycling magazine-fed weapons, in addition to many other physical tasks.

Semi-Autonomous Directed Payload Charges – “roller charges” in the colloquial – propel themselves around cover, through corridors, and across uneven terrain, seeking out and detonating near hostile targets. Armory legionnaires have taken to naming individual rollers, but mascot-attachment is unadvisable.

weapon.flavorDescription:
Much like GMS mechs, GMS weapons are reliable galactic standards, made using interchangeable parts and built to withstand almost any conditions. There are three lines currently in production.
The Type-III (T-3) line is made up of heavy weapons, ordnance, and other exotic, specialized, or massive weapons. A broad classification, the T-3 range includes conventional-kinetic anti-materiel rifles, super-rapid cycling pulse rifles, missile racks, cannons, and drone nexuses – “hive”style launchers that serve as miniature factory, hangar, and deployment systems for portable drones. The classification also includes the fearsome “segment knife”, a system that uses flash-printing to produce disposable edged weapons in a vicinity around the user.

The siege cannon is the Armory’s quintessential artillery piece: a howitzer cannon fed by an automated loading system. Typically mounted on long-range mechs deployed in artillery or squad-support roles, siege cannons are capable of both indirect and direct fire as the situation demands with variable ammunition options.

mechs.name: Sarcosuchus
mechs.notes:
-Cobbled and patched up with spare parts and scraps, given its own little flairs as a quiet "shove it" gesture, the chassis has been brought back to functioning and capable order. Junior assisted in installing the voice based notification system.

- 1 time use custom paint job system (little girl's drawing of a smiley face // pink)


Destroyed during an attempt to flee the scene, a miscalculated smoke bomb left the mech on its last, desperate strands, exposed.


The wreck recovered, he was given full attention for repairs once he was returned to the mech bay. Brought back to functioning and capable order, with proper acknowledgement and responsibility.
